---
title: "Final Project - "
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

setwd("~/Classes/BMI7235 - Machine Learning/Final Project")

library(NanoStringDiff)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tableone)

```

## Import Data
GEO Series Matrix files are not in a standard tabular format, so we will read it as lines and then parse it. The raw counts, normalized counts, and metadata are in Excel format, which we can read directly.


Adapted from the example code:
https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/NanoStringDiff/inst/doc/NanoStringDiff.R
And the documentation here:
https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/NanoStringDiff/inst/doc/NanoStringDiff.pdf

```{r}

# Import data
geo_series_lines <- readLines("GSE314577/GSE314577_series_matrix.txt")
raw_counts <- read_xlsx("GSE314577/GSE314577_VUMC_miRNA_raw_data.xlsx")
# normalized_counts <- read_xlsx("GSE314577/GSE314577_VUMC_miRNA_normalized_data.xlsx")
metadata <- read_xlsx("GSE314577/GSE314577_VUMC_Metadata_ch_revised.xlsx")

# Inspect the raw data structure
names(raw_counts)
head(raw_counts, 3)
names(metadata)
head(metadata, 3)

# Build NanoStringDiff input matrices from the raw miRNA table.
sample_id_col <- "ID"
miRNA_cols <- grep("^MIMAT", names(raw_counts), value = TRUE)
if (length(miRNA_cols) == 0) {
    stop("No MIMAT columns found in raw_counts.")
}

sample_ids <- as.character(raw_counts[[sample_id_col]])
count_mat <- as.matrix(raw_counts[, miRNA_cols])
rownames(count_mat) <- sample_ids

# NanoStringDiff expects features as rows and samples as columns.
endogenous <- t(count_mat)
colnames(endogenous) <- sample_ids

# If control probe columns are absent, use empty matrices as placeholders.
positive <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = ncol(endogenous))
negative <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = ncol(endogenous))
housekeeping <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = ncol(endogenous))
colnames(positive) <- colnames(endogenous)
colnames(negative) <- colnames(endogenous)
colnames(housekeeping) <- colnames(endogenous)
```

```{r}
# Build designs from metadata by matching the ID column to sample_ids.
metadata_cols <- names(metadata)
overlap_counts <- sapply(metadata_cols, function(col_name) {
    col_values <- metadata[[col_name]]
    if (is.factor(col_values) || is.character(col_values)) {
        sum(as.character(col_values) %in% sample_ids, na.rm = TRUE)
    } else {
        0
    }
})

id_col <- metadata_cols[which.max(overlap_counts)]
if (length(id_col) == 0 || overlap_counts[[id_col]] == 0) {
    stop("Could not find a metadata column that matches sample IDs.")
}

metadata_ids <- as.character(metadata[[id_col]])
designs <- metadata[match(sample_ids, metadata_ids), setdiff(metadata_cols, id_col), drop = FALSE]
designs <- as.data.frame(designs)
rownames(designs) <- sample_ids

```

```{r}
# Create NanoStringSet object

NanoStringDataset <- createNanoStringSet(
    endogenous,
    positive,
    negative,
    housekeeping,
    designs
)
NanoStringDataset
pData(NanoStringDataset)
head(exprs(NanoStringDataset))

```

```{r, include=FALSE}

# Quick sanity checks
dim(endogenous)
anyNA(endogenous)
any(duplicated(rownames(endogenous)))
any(duplicated(colnames(endogenous)))
all(colnames(endogenous) %in% rownames(designs))
sum(is.na(match(colnames(endogenous), rownames(designs))))
head(designs)

```
